violations.text: The presence of (rodent activity) is evidenced by several droppings observed in back storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by daily inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Pest control report must be provided to inspector before re-inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
The floors in the (kitchen) area is in poor repair. Several floor tiles observed cracked. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2026

The presence of (rodent activity) is not controlled as evidenced by several droppings observed in the kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment must contact a licensed PCS to perform a full evaluation/assessment of the facility. -Pest control report must be provided to inspector before the re-inspection date. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The floors in the (kitchen) area is in poor repair. Several floor tiles observed cracked. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2026

Establishment is lacking an allergy statement on their menu. An allergen statement shall be posted on menus and/or shall be made visible to consumers, alerting them to notify staff of any food allergies. i.e. "Before placing your order, please inform your server if a person in your party has a food allergy." New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The door to the outside, located in the (dry storage area), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The presence of (rodent activity) is not controlled as evidenced by several droppings observed in the kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment must contact a licensed PCS to perform a full evaluation/assessment of the facility. -Pest control report must be provided to inspector before the re-inspection date. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the (2-door refrig. shelves) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The floors in the (kitchen) area is in poor repair. Several floor tiles observed cracked. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2026

At the time of inspection, the tubing to the soda gun and ice plate were observed to be in contact with the ice in the ice well. The cold plate tubing and soda gun tube are to be kept separated from the ice to prevent possible contamination of the ice. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

At the time of inspection, the ice scoop was stored in the ice machine bin with the handle touching the ice. Utensils used to dispense food items are to be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container, or, stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Scoop adjusted for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

The non-food-contact surfaces of refrigeration and storage equipment in the kitchen area were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2024
The tile floor in the kitchen area was observed to be in poor repair (cracked/chipped). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025

The floors in the bar area is constructed of unsealed cement which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen/storage area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
